Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Datahäxan 53 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. WebPKI and You 45 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. About memory pressure, lock contention, and Data-oriented Design 48 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 237 upvotes, 53 comments
  5. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 147 upvotes, 33 comments
  6. Printf-Tac-Toe 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. Temporal: The 9-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 670 upvotes, 210 comments
  8. Against vibes: When is a generative model useful 83 upvotes, 19 comments
  9. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans 3519 upvotes, 1315 comments
  10. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 549 upvotes, 198 comments
  11. Show HN: s@: decentralized social networking over static sites 264 upvotes, 110 comments
  12. Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs would not be merged 231 upvotes, 102 comments
  13. BitNet: Inference framework for 1-bit LLMs 338 upvotes, 162 comments
  14. Iran-backed hackers claim wiper attack on medtech firm Stryker 153 upvotes, 74 comments
  15. 5,200 holes carved into a Peruvian mountain left by an ancient economy 127 upvotes, 63 comments
  16. Show HN: A context-aware permission guard for Claude Code 99 upvotes, 47 comments
  17. SBCL: A Sanely-Bootstrappable Common Lisp (2008) [pdf] 32 upvotes, 11 comments
  18. Faster asin() was hiding in plain sight 203 upvotes, 110 comments
  19. Google closes deal to acquire Wiz 289 upvotes, 170 comments
  20. Show HN: Klaus – OpenClaw on a VM, batteries included 141 upvotes, 80 comments
  21. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 24 upvotes, 9 comments
  22. Entities enabling scientific fraud at scale (2025) 288 upvotes, 196 comments
  23. NASA's DART spacecraft changed an asteroid's orbit around the sun 21 upvotes, 9 comments
  24. Returning to Rails in 2026 97 upvotes, 67 comments
  25. What Happens After You Die? (2016) 34 upvotes, 20 comments
  26. Personal Computer by Perplexity 152 upvotes, 123 comments
  27. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 298 upvotes, 264 comments
  28. Show HN: XLA-based array computing framework for R 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. The MacBook Neo 523 upvotes, 854 comments
